Title: ZmSSRP1, transactivated by OPAQUE11, positively regulates starch biosynthesis in maize endosperm

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing endosperm, peaking at 15 DAP; also expressed in leaves, pollen, and developing kernels
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmSSRP1 positively regulates starch biosynthesis in maize endosperm. 2. CRISPR/Cas9 knockout mutants showed smaller starch granules and reduced total starch, amylose, and amylopectin. 3. Overexpression increased starch granule size and increased amylose and amylopectin accumulation. 4. Loss of ZmSSRP1 downregulated multiple starch synthesis-related genes.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate contributing to kernel yield and quality
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CBM48 domain-containing protein with four low-complexity regions
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 21-DAP endosperm, 3 biological replicates per genotype; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for increasing kernel starch content and grain yield/quality; overexpression or promoter/cis-element editing could fine-tune starch accumulation in elite hybrid parents
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for subcellular localization and overexpression constructs
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RT-qPCR; SEM/TEM; starch/amylose/amylopectin quantification; RNA-seq
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologue of rice OsFLO6. Direct protein interactions with ZmGBSS1, ZmISA1, and ZmSS4 were tested by LCI/BiFC in tobacco/protoplasts but no detectable positive signal was observed.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing endosperm, peaking at 15 DAP
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout caused shrunken kernels, reduced kernel thickness and 100-grain weight, increased soluble and reducing sugars, and increased crude protein; overexpression effects on total starch were limited except amylose increase
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of starch, including both amylose and amylopectin, and increases starch granule size in maize endosperm

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assays in maize protoplasts and tobacco leaves; mutant and overexpression analysis; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch, amylose, and amylopectin accumulation and increases starch granule size, at least partly through activation of ZmSSRP1
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate contributing to kernel development, yield, and quality
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss-of-function mutant showed reduced endosperm size, lower starch and crude protein accumulation, and smaller starch granules
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O11 binds the ZmSSRP1 promoter and activates its transcription. 2. o11 mutants showed reduced starch, amylose, and amylopectin, while O11 overexpression showed the opposite phenotype. 3. Double-mutant analysis supports that O11 and ZmSSRP1 act in the same or overlapping pathway regulating endosperm starch synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endosperm-specific transcription factor active during endosperm development
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving maize kernel starch content and quality; promoter or cis-element editing may tune starch accumulation for breeding
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive upstream regulator in the O11-ZmSSRP1 module controlling endosperm starch biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter used in dual-luciferase effector constructs and overexpression constructs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ZmSSRP1 transcription by binding its promoter; positively affects expression of starch synthesis-related genes including ZmGBSSs and ZmISAs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EMSA did not show in vitro band shift despite positive Y1H and dual-LUC evidence, possibly due to missing modifications or sequence-context effects. O11 was previously known to regulate other endosperm and nutrient metabolism TFs, but those are background/context genes here.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Starch biosynthesis in endosperm via transcriptional regulation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: W22; O11-OE line; control line as reported
